projectDescription: A zero-dependency, modular JavaScript toolkit with four modules — DOM sugar (DiWu), utilities (TianFeng), async testing (FireWyrm), and logging/benchmarking (ShuiHu) — designed to fill the gaps in vanilla JS without heavy frameworks.
keyFeatures: DiWu (dw): Chainable, prototype-extending DOM manipulation with WeakMap-backed event registry, Proxy-based class/attribute access, and Web Animations API integration

TianFeng (tf): Comprehensive utility layer covering DOM querying, hash-based SPA routing, reactive stores, localStorage persistence, throttle, async helpers, fetch wrappers, form validation/conforming, and device detection
FireWyrm (fw): Minimalist async-safe testing framework with chainable assertions (25+ matchers), section grouping, sequential test queue, and object method mocking/restore
ShuiHu (sh): Browser-focused logging with four severity levels (debug/warn/error/dire), Proxy-based step benchmarking using Uint32Array, in-memory log history, and DOM log holder
UMD build: works in browsers via CDN script tags, ES module imports, CommonJS (Node), and AMD/RequireJS
publicApiSurface: setTask(fn, delay?, args?, iterations?) / clearTask(id) / changeTask(id, opts)
fetchJson(url, ops?) / fetchText(url, ops?) / fetchFresh(url, ops?)
route.init(startUrl?) / route.when(url, cb) / route.go(url) / route.guard(cb) / route.back() / route.forward()
fw.start() / fw.section(name) / fw.test(statement, fn) / fw.assert(statement, value).<matcher>()
